| First game       = ''Unofficial''<br />{{fb|MDA|1990}} 2–4 {{fb-rt|GEO|1990}} <br /> ([[Chișinău]], [[Soviet Union]]; 2 July 1991)<br />''Official''<br />{{fb|USA}} 1–1 {{fb-rt|MDA|1990}} <br /> ([[Jacksonville]], [[United States]]; 16 April 1994)
| Largest win      = {{fb|PAK}} [[1992 Jordan International Tournament#Group A|0–5]] {{fb-rt|MDA|1990}} <br /> ([[Amman]], [[Jordan]]; 18 August 1992)
| Largest loss     = {{fb|NOR}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group I|11–1]] {{fb-rt|MDA}} <br /> ([[Oslo]], [[Norway]]; 9 September 2025)
|website={{Official website|1=https://fmf.md/home/nationale/masculin-nationale/echipa-nationala-1582999347/lot?lang=en |name=fmf.md}} {{in lang|ro}}
}}

The '''Moldova national football team''' ({{langx|ro|Echipa națională de fotbal a Moldovei}}) represents [[Moldova]] in men's international [[Association football|football]] and is administered by the [[Moldovan Football Federation]], the governing body for [[football in Moldova]]. Moldova's home ground is [[Zimbru Stadium]] in [[Chișinău]]. Shortly before the break-up of the [[Soviet Union]], they played their first match against [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]] on 2 July 1991.

Two of their three best results in the 2020s came during the [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying|qualifiers]] for [[UEFA Euro 2024]], with a win over [[Poland national football team|Poland]] (3–2) in Chișinău, coming back from a 0–2 deficit, and a draw against [[Czech Republic national football team|Czech Republic]] (0–0) in Chișinău. In 2007, Moldova upset [[Hungary national football team|Hungary]] 3–0 in Chișinău in [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying|Euro 2008 qualifying]]. Another notable result was a 5–2 win over [[Montenegro national football team|Montenegro]] during [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA)|2014 FIFA World Cup qualifying]]. The team has never qualified for the final stages of the [[UEFA European Championship]] nor the [[FIFA World Cup]] since first entering qualifying in 1994.

Following Moldova's 4–0 defeat to [[England national football team|England]] in September 1997, British writer and comedian [[Tony Hawks]] travelled to Moldova to challenge and beat all 11 Moldovan international footballers at tennis. The feature film version of the book of the same name, ''Playing the Moldovans at Tennis'', was filmed in and around Chișinău in May and June 2010 and was released in the spring of 2012.

==History==
===1990s===
The Moldovan Football Federation was founded in 1990. However, the national football team of Moldova did not play an official international match until 1991, when Moldova lost 4–2 to [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]]. [[Alexandru Spiridon]] was the first player to score a goal in the team's history.

Moldova entered the [[1992 Jordan International Tournament]], a tournament consisting of friendly matches. Moldova obtained its biggest win to date, a 5–0 victory against [[Pakistan national football team|Pakistan]], in which [[Serghei Alexandrov]] scored four goals, this being Moldova's first hat-trick. Moldova's second hat-trick was scored by [[Iurie Miterev]] in a 3–1 win against [[Congo national football team|Congo]] in the same tournament.<ref>{{cite web|first=Hassanin|last= Mubarak|title=Jordan International Tournament 1992|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesj/jordan-tourn92.html|website=[[RSSSF]]|date=8 August 2002|access-date=27 June 2024}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|first=Neil|last= Morrison |title=1992 matches: Intercontinental (July–December)|url=https://www.rsssf.org/intldetails/1992ic2.html|website=[[RSSSF]]|date=31 October 2019|access-date=27 June 2024}}</ref>

Moldova became a [[UEFA]] member in 1993 and a [[FIFA]] member in 1994.<ref>{{cite web | language=ro |title=FEDERAȚIA MOLDOVENEASCĂ DE FOTBAL |url=https://fmf.md/home/fmf/despre-fmf/informatii-generale-446 |website=fmf.md |publisher=fmf |access-date=26 May 2024}}</ref> Moldova's first match recognised by FIFA was a 1–1 draw against the [[USA men's national soccer team|United States]]. Moldova's first attempt to qualify for an international tournament was in [[UEFA Euro 1996 qualifying Group 7|UEFA Euro 1996 qualifying]], its first competitive match being a 1–0 win over Georgia. The Moldovans beat Georgia twice and [[Wales national football team|Wales]] once, placing 4th out of 6 and failing to qualify.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/96e.html |title=European Championship 1996 |website=[[RSSSF]] |publisher=[[Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation]] |date=18 January 2000|access-date=27 June 2024}}</ref> Moldova's following qualifying campaign, their first attempt to qualify for the [[FIFA World Cup]], was the [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 2|1998 qualifiers]], but they lost all matches, placing last in the group.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA) 1998, football - table and standings |url=https://soccer365.me/competitions/739/1998/ |website=soccer365.me |access-date=27 June 2024}}</ref> Moldova also placed last in [[UEFA Euro 2000 qualifying Group 3|UEFA Euro 2000 qualifying]]. The Moldovans obtained 4 draws, two with [[Northern Ireland national football team|Northern Ireland]], one each with [[Turkey national football team|Turkey]] and [[Finland national football team|Finland]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tables/00e.html |title=European Championship 2000 |website=[[RSSSF]] |publisher=[[Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation]] |date=17 January 2004 |access-date=27 June 2024}}</ref>

===2000s===
In [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 4|2002 FIFA World Cup qualification]] Moldova lost 6–0 against [[Sweden men's national football team|Sweden]], which stood as its biggest defeat for almost 20 years. Moldova was the second-last team in the group above [[Azerbaijan national football team|Azerbaijan]], which it beat 2–0.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA) 2002, football - table and standings |url=https://soccer365.me/competitions/739/2002/ |website=soccer365.me |access-date=27 June 2024}}</ref> In [[UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying Group 3|UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying]] the Moldovans beat [[Austria national football team|Austria]] and [[Belarus national football team|Belarus]], placing above the latter in the group.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Burkert |first1=Sturmius |last2=Sivritepe |first2=Erdinç |url=http://www.RSSSF.com/tables/04e.html |title=European Championship 2004 |website=RSSSF.com |publisher=[[Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation]] |date=7 July 2004 |access-date=27 June 2024}}</ref> Moldova finished last in [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 5|2006 FIFA World Cup qualification]], however it drew with [[Norway national football team|Norway]] and [[Scotland national football team|Scotland]].<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA) 2006, football - table and standings |url=https://soccer365.me/competitions/739/2006/ |website=soccer365.me |access-date=27 June 2024}}</ref>

In a 2006 friendly against [[Lithuania national football team|Lithuania]], [[Serghei Cleșcenco]] scored his 11th and last goal, remaining Moldova's top goalscorer until 2023.<ref>{{cite web | url = http://eu-football.info/_player.php?id=10721 | title = Football PLAYER: Serghei Cleşcenco | publisher = eu-football.info | accessdate = 27 June 2024}}</ref> In [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying]] Moldova finished fifth in the seven-team [[UEFA Euro 2008 qualifying Group C|Group C]] with 12 points, beating [[Bosnia and Herzegovina national football team|Bosnia and Herzegovina]] 1–0 and Hungary 3–0.<ref>{{cite web |title=UEFA EURO 2008 Standings |website=UEFA |url=http://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/season=2008/standings/round=2241/group=2630 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180709191931/http://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/season=2008/standings/round=2241/group=2630/index.html |archive-date=9 July 2018}}</ref> Following these qualifiers Moldova placed 37th in April 2008 [[FIFA Men's World Ranking|FIFA Ranking]], the highest position it ever reached.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA World Ranking as of Apr 2008 |url=https://en.fifaranking.net/ranking/index.php?d=2008-04-09 |website=FIFARanking.net | date=9 April 2008 }}</ref> In [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group 2|2010 FIFA World Cup qualification]] the Moldovans placed last in the group. They drew with [[Greece national football team|Greece]] once and [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]] twice.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA) 2010, football - table and standings |url=https://soccer365.me/competitions/739/2010/ |website=soccer365.me |access-date=27 June 2024}}</ref> Between the games for these qualifiers Moldova beat [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]] 4–1 in [[Yerevan]] in a friendly match.<ref>{{cite web |title=Armenia vs. Moldova 1 - 4 |url=https://int.soccerway.com/matches/2009/08/12/world/friendlies/armenia/moldova/826321/ |website=Soccerway}}</ref>

===2010s===
[[File:AUT vs. MDA 2015-09-05 (004).jpg|thumb|245x245px|The Moldova line-up before the [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying Group C|Euro 2016 qualifying]] match against [[Austria national football team|Austria]], September 2015]]
Moldova beat [[Finland national football team|Finland]] 2–0 in [[UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying Group E|Euro 2012 qualifying]], placing only above [[San Marino national football team|San Marino]].<ref>{{cite web|title=UEFA Euro 2012 qualifying Group E|url=https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/history/seasons/2012/qualifying/|work=[[UEFA]]|publisher=Union of European Football Associations|archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20200604140821/https://www.uefa.com/uefaeuro/history/seasons/2012/qualifying/|archive-date=4 June 2020}}</ref> The Moldovans finished only above San Marino again in [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group H|2014 FIFA World Cup qualifying]], but they beat Montenegro 5–2 and drew with [[Ukraine national football team|Ukraine]] and Poland.<ref name="soccer365 UEFA 2014">{{cite web |title=FIFA World Cup qualification (UEFA) 2014, football - table and standings |url=https://soccer365.me/competitions/739/2014/ |website=soccer365.me |access-date=28 June 2024}}</ref> During [[UEFA Euro 2016 qualifying]] Moldova achieved a 1–1 draw against [[Russia national football team|Russia]] at [[Moscow]]. The equalising goal was [[Alexandru Epureanu]]'s last international goal, who went on to become the first Moldovan with 100 appearances for the national team.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://eu-football.info/_player.php?id=5500|title=Alexandru Epureanu|website=eu-football.info|access-date=28 June 2024}}</ref> In the same competition, Moldova suffered a humiliating 0–1 defeat against the [[Liechtenstein national football team|Liechtenstein]] on home ground, placing last in the group. In [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification – UEFA Group D|2018 FIFA World Cup qualification]] the Moldovans placed last in the group again, only obtaining two draws with Georgia.

In the inaugural season of the [[UEFA Nations League]], the Moldovans played in [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League D|League D]], the competition's lowest division. They placed third in their group, above San Marino. Moldova's victory over them was its first competitive win since 2014. In [[UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying Group H|UEFA Euro 2020 qualifying]] Moldova suffered another disappointing result, a 0–1 loss to [[Andorra national football team|Andorra]], finishing last in the group.

===2020s===
Moldova was the best third-placed team of [[2018–19 UEFA Nations League D]] and obtained promotion to [[2020–21 UEFA Nations League C]] due to a revamp of the competition's format. Moldova obtained only one point, a draw with the [[Kosovo national football team|Kosovo]], placing last in its group. Thus, Moldova had to play the relegation play-outs. Between the matches for the 2020–21 UEFA Nations League, Moldova suffered a 6–0 defeat to [[Italy national football team|Italy]] in a friendly match, which equalled Moldova's worst defeat at the time.<ref>{{cite web |title=Italy vs. Moldova 6 - 0 |url=https://int.soccerway.com/matches/2020/10/07/world/friendlies/italy/moldova/3287336/ |website=Soccerway}}</ref>

In [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification]] Moldova obtained only one point, a draw with the [[Faroe Islands national football team|Faroe Islands]]. Moldova also suffered what was at the time its worst defeat ever during these qualifiers, an 8–0 loss against [[Denmark national football team|Denmark]]. During these qualifiers Moldova placed 181st in October 2021 [[FIFA Men's World Ranking|FIFA Ranking]], the lowest position it ever reached.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA national team rankings |url=https://football-ranking.com/fifa-rankings?period=21+October+2021&page=4 |website=football-ranking.com}}</ref> After losing to [[Kazakhstan national football team|Kazakhstan]] on penalties in 2020–21 UEFA Nations League relegation play-outs, the Moldovans were relegated to [[2022–23 UEFA Nations League D]]. Moldova was tied on points with [[Latvia national football team|Latvia]], but because of a lower goal difference placed second, failing to achieve promotion.

The [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying]] was a watershed moment for Moldova. The Moldovans came agonisingly close to a historic Euro debut, acquiring ten points in a [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying Group E|group of five]]; the Moldovans also gained an impressive feat by being undefeated at home throughout the qualification, including a historic shock 3–2 comeback win over [[Poland national football team|Poland]]<ref>{{Cite news |title=Moldova, ranked 171st, stun Poland in comeback win |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/65907771 |access-date=2024-01-20 |work=BBC Sport |language=en-GB}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Moldova 3 - 2 Poland {{!}} Football |url=http://www.theguardian.com//football/match/2023/jun/20/moldova-v-poland |access-date=2024-01-20 |website=The Guardian |language=en}}</ref> and were one win away from qualifying for [[UEFA Euro 2024]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Chan |first=Marcus |date=2023-11-20 |title=Nation ranked 157th in the world could qualify for Euro 2024 despite only winning two games |url=https://www.sportbible.com/football/football-news/euro-2024-qualifying-round-uefa-rankings-748434-20231120 |access-date=2024-01-20 |website=SPORTbible |language=en}}</ref> However, a denting away loss to the [[Czech Republic national football team|Czech Republic]] in the final matchday killed off Moldova's qualification hopes, as they finished fourth and were eliminated due to an inferior record.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2023-11-20 |title=Northern Ireland stun Denmark, Czech coach quits after sealing Euro 2024 spot |url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2023/nov/20/northern-ireland-denmark-czech-republic-jaroslav-silhavy |access-date=2024-01-20 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |agency=Reuters |date=2023-11-21 |title=Czech Republic qualifies for Euro 2024 with Moldova win but coach resigns |url=https://sportstar.thehindu.com/football/czech-republic-qualifies-euro-2024-coach-jaroslav-silhavy-resigns/article67556706.ece |access-date=2024-01-20 |website=Sportstar |language=en}}</ref> [[Ion Nicolaescu]] scored his 11th and 12th international goals in the 3–2 win against Poland, becoming Moldova's top goal scorer. With 4 goals in the competition, he became the first Moldovan to be the top goal scorer of a qualifying group.<ref>{{cite web |title=Superlativele anului fotbalistic 2023 |url=https://fmf.md/noutate/8964/superlativele-anului-fotbalistic-2023 |website=fmf.md |date=31 December 2023|access-date=28 June 2024}}</ref>

On 9 September 2025, Moldova suffered their biggest defeat ever in a 11–1 loss against [[Norway national football team|Norway]] at the [[Ullevaal Stadion|Ullevaal Stadium]] in [[Oslo]], [[Norway]], where [[Erling Haaland]] scored 5 goals in this match. Two days after the match, [[Serghei Cleșcenco]] resigned as Moldova's head coach.<ref>{{cite web|date=11 September 2025|publisher=[[Moldovan Football Federation]]|access-date=11 September 2025|title=Serghei Cleșcenco și-a depus mandatul de selecționer|language=ro|url=https://fmf.md/noutate/14866/serghei-clescenco-sia-depus-mandatul-de-selectioner}}</ref>

==Current sponsorship==
Official sponsors: [[maib]], [[Mastercard]], [[Orange Group|Orange]], [[Kaufland]], [[Jako]], [[Cricova (winery)|Cricova]], OM, Invitro Diagnostics, Estate Invest Company.

=== Kit suppliers ===
{| class="wikitable"
!Kit supplier
!Period
|-
|{{Flagicon|Germany}} [[Reusch (company)|Reusch]]
|1994–1997
|-
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Puma (brand)|Puma]]
|1998–2000
|-
|{{flagicon|GER}} [[Jako]]
|2001–present
|}

== Home stadium ==
[[File:Zimbru Stadium 2023.jpg|thumb|Zimbru Stadium]]
The Moldova national football team plays its official games at [[Zimbru Stadium]] in Chișinău. The stadium is a privately held structure, but is under administration of FMF until 2026.<ref>{{cite web |title=Stadionul Zimbru |url=https://fmf.md/home/fmf/despre-fmf/stadionul-zimbru-3d-1583076504 |website=fmf.md |language=ro}}</ref> The senior team, along with youth teams use [[Stadionul CPSM]] for training purposes. It is located in [[Vadul lui Vodă]], a town along the [[Dniester River]], approximately 25 kilometres from [[Chișinău]].

From 1991 to 2006, Moldova played its matches at the [[Republican Stadium (Chișinău)|Republican Stadium]] in Chișinău. The team also played its home matches in [[UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying Group 3|UEFA Euro 2004 qualifying]] and some friendlies, the latest in 2013, at [[Sheriff Arena]] in [[Tiraspol]].

===2026===
{{football box collapsible|format=1

==Coaching staff==
===Current coaching staff===
{{Fb cs header}}
{{Fb cs staff |bg=|p=Head coach|s={{flagicon|MDA}} [[Lilian Popescu]]}}
{{Fb cs staff |bg=|p=Assistant coach|s={{flagicon|GEO}} [[Shota Makharadze]]}}
{{Fb cs staff |bg=|p=Coach analyst|s={{flagicon|MDA}} [[Cristian Efros]]}}
{{Fb cs staff |bg=|p=Goalkeeping coach|s={{flagicon|MDA}} [[Vladislav Baklanov]]}}
{{Fb cs staff |bg=|p=Fitness coach|s={{flagicon|MDA}} [[Denis Zmeu]]}}
{{Fb cs footer|u=7 October 2025 |s=[https://fmf.md/home/nationale/masculin-nationale/echipa-nationala-1582999347/stuff-tehnic]|date=October 2025}}

===Manager history===
Former Moldova international [[Lilian Popescu]] is the current head coach of Moldova since September 2025.<ref>{{cite web |title=Lilian Popescu, noul selecționer al Moldovei |url=https://fmf.md/noutate/14918/lilian-popescu-noul-selectioner-al-moldovei |website=fmf.md |publisher=[[Moldovan Football Federation]] |language=Romanian |date=16 September 2025}}</ref>

:''As of 9 June 2026''<ref>{{cite web|title=Moldova national team managers|url=http://eu-football.info/_managers.php?im=138|publisher=eu-football.info|access-date=25 September 2022|archive-date=5 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170805140500/https://eu-football.info/_managers.php?im=138|url-status=live}}</ref>

== Players ==

===Current squad===
The following players were selected for the friendly matches against [[Bulgaria national football team|Bulgaria]] and [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]] on 5 and 9 June 2026, respectively.<ref>{{cite web |title= Naționala. Lotul final pentru meciurile din luna noiembrie |url= https://fmf.md/noutate/15511/nationala-lotul-final-pentru-meciurile-din-luna-noiembrie |website=FMD |access-date=12 November 2025}}</ref>

''Caps and goals are correct as of 9 June 2026, after the match against [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=[[Emil Tîmbur]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|7|21|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[FC Sheriff Tiraspol|Sheriff Tiraspol]]|clubnat=MDA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=12|pos=GK|name=[[Dumitru Celeadnic]]|age={{birth date and age|1992|4|23|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[FC Kyzylzhar|Kyzylzhar]]|clubnat=KAZ}}
{{nat fs g player|no=23|pos=GK|name=[[Andrei Cojuhar]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|7|20|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[NK Veres Rivne|Veres Rivne]]|clubnat=UKR}}
{{nat fs break}}

{{nat fs end}}
===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Moldova squad within the last 12 months.<ref>{{cite news |date=18 November 2024 |title=Naționala. Imagini de la ultimul antrenament al tricolorilor înaintea amicalului cu Gibraltar |url=https://fmf.md/noutate/11651/nationala-imagini-de-la-ultimul-antrenament-al-tricolorilor-inaintea-amicalului-cu-gibraltar |access-date=18 November 2024 |publisher=FMF |language=ro}}</ref>

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Cristian Dros]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|4|15|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[KF Vllaznia|Vllaznia]]|clubnat=ALB|latest=v.{{fb|NOR}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Vitalie Damașcan]]|age={{Birth date and age|1999|1|24|df=y}}|caps=46|goals=5|club=[[Hapoel Jerusalem F.C.|Hapoel Jerusalem]]|clubnat=ISR|latest=v.{{fb|BUL}}, 5 June 2026 <sup>INJ</sup>}}
{{nat fs r player|no=11|pos=FW|name=[[Alexandru Boiciuc]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|8|21|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[FC Bihor Oradea (2022)|Bihor Oradea]]|clubnat=ROU|latest=v.{{fb|CYP}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=25|pos=FW|name=[[Nicolae Rotaru (footballer)|Nicolae Rotaru]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|12|19|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Petrocub Hîncești|Petrocub Hîncești]]|clubnat=MDA|latest=v.{{fb|CYP}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Ion Nicolaescu]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|9|7|df=y}}|caps=56|goals=18|club=[[Maccabi Tel Aviv F.C.|Maccabi Tel Aviv]]|clubnat=ISR|latest=v.{{fb|ISR}}, 16 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mihai Lupan]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|9|8|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Petrocub Hîncești|Petrocub Hîncești]]|clubnat=MDA|latest=v.{{fb|EST}}, 14 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Maxim Cojocaru]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|1|13|df=y}}|caps=32|goals=1|club=[[FC Petrocub Hîncești|Petrocub Hîncești]]|clubnat=MDA|latest=v.{{fb|NOR}}, 9 September 2025}}

{{nat fs break}}
<sup>INJ</sup> Withdrew due to injury<br />
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad / standby<br />
<sup>RET</sup> Retired from the national team<br />
<sup>SUS</sup> Serving suspension<br />
<sup>WD</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to non-injury issue.
{{nat fs end}}

==Individual records==
{{main|List of Moldova international footballers}}

{{updated|9 June 2026}}<ref>{{cite web|title=Most Moldova caps|url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=138&data=9|website=EU Football|access-date=25 September 2022|archive-date=28 September 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220928221235/https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=138&data=9|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|title=Top Moldova Goal Scorers|url=https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=138&data=6|website=EU Football|access-date=25 September 2022|archive-date=24 October 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221024142241/https://eu-football.info/_players.php?id=138&data=6|url-status=live}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Moldova.''

|{{flagicon|Europe}} [[UEFA Euro 2020|2020]]{{efn|The tournament was held in 11 cities in 11 UEFA countries ([[Azerbaijan]], [[Denmark]], [[England]], [[Germany]], [[Hungary]], [[Italy]], [[Netherlands]], [[Romania]], [[Russia]], [[Scotland]], and [[Spain]]). It was originally scheduled to take place from 12 June to 12 July 2020 but was postponed to 11 June and 11 July 2021.}}

==Head-to-head record==
{{updated|9 June 2026, after the match against [[Armenia national football team|Armenia]].}}<ref name=rsssf>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/mold-intres.html|title=Moldova - International results|first1=Barrie|last1=Courtney|website=[[RSSSF]]|access-date=3 October 2022|archive-date=5 October 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221005145253/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/mold-intres.html|url-status=live}}</ref>
{| bgcolor="#f7f8ff" cellpadding="3" cellspacing="0" border="1" style="font-size: 95%; border: grey solid 1px; border-collapse: collapse;text-align:center;"
|+'''Key'''
| style="background: #bbf3bb;" width="20" |
| bgcolor="#ffffff" align="left" | '''Positive balance''' (more Wins)
|-
| style="background: #ffffbb;" width="20" |
| bgcolor="#ffffff" align="left" | '''Neutral balance''' (Wins = Losses)
|-
| style="background: #ffbbbb;" width="20" |
| bgcolor="#ffffff" align="left" | '''Negative balance''' (more Losses)
|}

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;
